response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which makes his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David gathered three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together they have 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. The total number of blueberries they have is 76.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch of muffins needed 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make they divided the total number of blueberries by the number required per batch. This calculation gives 8 batches with 4 blueberries left over. They sold each batch for $5. Therefore the total money they earned from selling the batches is 40 dollars.

***

The leftover blueberries were not used for making muffins. They decided to keep them for future use. The money earned from selling the muffins was 40 dollars. They used their blueberries to make 8 batches of muffins. Each batch was sold for $5 which helped them earn money for their efforts.

***

The total money they made from their blueberries was 40 dollars. They used their blueberries to make muffins and sold them for a good price. The money they earned helped support their youth.
